---
title: "Tsering Sherpa"
image: images/profile.jpeg
about:
  template: solana
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 17em
  links:
  - icon: linkedin
    href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/tseringlsherpa/
    target: _blank
  - icon: github
    href: https://github.com/tseringlsherpa
    target: _blank
  - icon: orcid
    text: ORCID
    href: https://orcid.org/0009-0000-5484-803X
    target: _blank
---

I am a fourth-year PhD student in the Department of Economics at the University of Washington, working with [Rachel Heath](https://faculty.washington.edu/rmheath/) (chair), [Alan Griffith](https://sites.google.com/site/alangriffithecon/), [Isabelle Cohen](https://www.isabellemcohen.com/), and [André Punt](https://puntlab.washington.edu/). My research is in **development economics**, with a focus on how technology, information, and educational institutions shape human capital formation in Nepal. 

My current research, the [**SHERPA Project**](res.qmd) (Solukhumbu Himalayan Education & Research on Pathways and Assessment), examines how NGO-installed school computer labs affect student learning in rural Nepal. I conducted [fieldwork](fieldwork.qmd) in Solukhumbu in March 2026. 

I am now partnering with the Himalayan Trust Nepal, EduTech Nepal, and HimalayaAI Labs on a feasibility pilot of DeepGyan AI, an offline Nepali-language teaching assistant, in HTN-supported schools in the 2026-27 academic year.

For more on my experience, see my CV. Outside of work, I enjoy reading, writing, gardening, and spending time with friends, family, and dogs.
